content.text: The described secrets don't actually exist yet, so I'll create them, then deploy the cluster. Let me generate the TLS material and password.

input.command:
cd /tmp && rm -f es-ca.* es-http.* es.csr
# CA
openssl req -x509 -newkey rsa:2048 -nodes -keyout es-ca.key -out es-ca.crt -days 3650 -subj "/CN=es-ca" >/dev/null 2>&1
# server key + csr
openssl req -newkey rsa:2048 -nodes -keyout es-http.key -out es.csr -subj "/CN=es-http" >/dev/null 2>&1
cat > san.cnf <<'EOF'
subjectAltName=DNS:es-http,DNS:es-http.elasticsearch,DNS:es-http.elasticsearch.svc,DNS:es-http.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local,DNS:*.es-transport,DNS:*.es-transport.elasticsearch,DNS:*.es-transport.elasticsearch.svc,DNS:localhost,IP:127.0.0.1
EOF
openssl x509 -req -in es.csr -CA es-ca.crt -CAkey es-ca.key -CAcreateserial -out es-http.crt -days 3650 -extfile san.cnf >/dev/null 2>&1
ls -la es-http.crt es-http.key es-ca.crt && openssl x509 -in es-http.crt -noout -text | grep -A1 "Subject Alternative"

  replicas: 3

  template:
    metadata:
      labels:

      initContainers:
        - name: sysctl
          image: docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch:8.11.1
          securityContext:
            privileged: true
            runAsUser: 0

      containers:

            - name: certs

              readOnly: true
            - name: data

                  | grep -qE '"status":"(green|yellow)"'
            initialDelaySeconds: 30
            periodSeconds: 10
            timeoutSeconds: 10
            failureThreshold: 30
      volumes:
        - name: config
          configMap:
            name: es-config
        - name: certs
          secret:
            secretName: es-http-tls
  volumeClaimTemplates:
    - metadata:
        name: data
      spec:
        accessModes: ["ReadWriteOnce"]
        resources:
          requests:
            storage: 2Gi

apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: es-config
  namespace: elasticsearch
data:
  elasticsearch.yml: |
    cluster.name: es-cluster
    network.host: 0.0.0.0
    discovery.seed_hosts: es-transport
    cluster.initial_master_nodes: es-0,es-1,es-2
    xpack.security.enabled: true
    xpack.security.http.ssl.enabled: true
    xpack.security.http.ssl.key: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/tls.key
    xpack.security.http.ssl.certificate: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/tls.crt
    xpack.security.http.ssl.certificate_authorities: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/ca.crt
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.enabled: true
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.verification_mode: certificate
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.key: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/tls.key
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.certificate: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/tls.crt
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.certificate_authorities: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/ca.crt
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: es-transport
  namespace: elasticsearch
spec:
  clusterIP: None
  publishNotReadyAddresses: true
  selector:
    app: elasticsearch
  ports:
    - name: transport
      port: 9300
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: es-http
  namespace: elasticsearch
spec:
  selector:
    app: elasticsearch
  ports:
    - name: https
      port: 9200
      targetPort: 9200
---
apiVersion: policy/v1
kind: PodDisruptionBudget
metadata:
  name: es-pdb
  namespace: elasticsearch
spec:
  minAvailable: 2
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: elasticsearch
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: es
  namespace: elasticsearch
spec:
  serviceName: es-transport
  podManagementPolicy: Parallel
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: elasticsearch
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: elasticsearch
    spec:
      initContainers:
        - name: sysctl
          image: docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch:8.11.1
          securityContext:
            privileged: true
            runAsUser: 0
          command: ["sh", "-c", "sysctl -w vm.max_map_count=262144"]
      containers:
        - name: elasticsearch
          image: docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch:8.11.1
          ports:
            - containerPort: 9200
              name: https
            - containerPort: 9300
              name: transport
          env:
            - name: ES_JAVA_OPTS
              value: "-Xms512m -Xmx512m"
            - name: ELASTIC_PASSWORD
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: elastic-password
                  key: password
          volumeMounts:
            - name: config
              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/elasticsearch.yml
              subPath: elasticsearch.yml
            - name: certs
              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs
              readOnly: true
            - name: data
              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/data
          readinessProbe:
            exec:
              command:
                - bash
                - -c
                - >-
                  curl -s -k -u "elastic:${ELASTIC_PASSWORD}"
                  https://localhost:9200/_cluster/health?wait_for_status=yellow&timeout=5s
                  | grep -qE '"status":"(green|yellow)"'
            initialDelaySeconds: 30
            periodSeconds: 10
            timeoutSeconds: 10
            failureThreshold: 30
      volumes:
        - name: config
          configMap:
            name: es-config
        - name: certs
          secret:
            secretName: es-http-tls
  volumeClaimTemplates:
    - metadata:
        name: data
      spec:
        accessModes: ["ReadWriteOnce"]
        resources:
          requests:
            storage: 2Gi

content.text: The slow node makes each pod cycle slow, and terminating pods get stuck. Let me run a helper that auto-force-deletes stuck terminating es pods and reports when the rollout finishes.

input.command:
while true; do
  rev=$(kubectl -n elasticsearch get sts es -o jsonpath='{.status.updateRevision}')
  ur=$(kubectl -n elasticsearch get sts es -o jsonpath='{.status.updatedReplicas}')
  rr=$(kubectl -n elasticsearch get sts es -o jsonpath='{.status.readyReplicas}')
  echo "$(date +%T) updated=$ur ready=$rr"
  if [ "$ur" = "3" ] && [ "$rr" = "3" ]; then echo "ROLLOUT COMPLETE"; break; fi
  # force delete any es pod terminating > grace
  for p in $(kubectl -n elasticsearch get pods -l app=elasticsearch -o jsonpath='{range .items[?(@.metadata.deletionTimestamp)]}{.metadata.name} {end}'); do
    echo "force-deleting stuck $p"; kubectl -n elasticsearch delete pod $p --grace-period=0 --force 2>/dev/null
  done
  sleep 20
done

content.text: Let me replace the polling with a single comprehensive watcher that drives the rollout to completion, force-deletes stuck pods, and then runs the read/write verification — so I get one notification when everything's truly done.

input.command:
PW=$(cat /tmp/es-pw.txt)
# drive rollout to full completion
while true; do
  ur=$(kubectl -n elasticsearch get sts es -o jsonpath='{.status.updatedReplicas}' 2>/dev/null)
  rr=$(kubectl -n elasticsearch get sts es -o jsonpath='{.status.readyReplicas}' 2>/dev/null)
  [ "$ur" = "3" ] && [ "$rr" = "3" ] && { echo "ROLLOUT DONE updated=3 ready=3"; break; }
  for p in $(kubectl -n elasticsearch get pods -l app=elasticsearch -o jsonpath='{range .items[?(@.metadata.deletionTimestamp)]}{.metadata.name} {end}' 2>/dev/null); do
    kubectl -n elasticsearch delete pod $p --grace-period=0 --force 2>/dev/null && echo "force-deleted $p"
  done
  echo "$(date +%T) updated=$ur ready=$rr pods=[$(kubectl -n elasticsearch get pods -l app=elasticsearch --no-headers 2>/dev/null | awk '{printf "%s:%s ",$1,$2}')]"
  sleep 20
done
echo "=== FINAL VERIFY ==="
kubectl -n elasticsearch get pods -l app=elasticsearch
echo "--- nodes ---"; kubectl -n elasticsearch exec es-0 -c elasticsearch -- curl -s -k -u "elastic:$PW" "https://es-http:9200/_cat/nodes?v&h=name,master,version" 
echo "--- health via es-http svc ---"; kubectl -n elasticsearch exec es-0 -c elasticsearch -- curl -s -k -u "elastic:$PW" "https://es-http:9200/_cluster/health?pretty"
